---
title: "one-piece-data"
author: "John Esparragoza"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Libraries

```{r}
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
```


## loading up the One piece API

```{r}
# devil fruits
characters_url <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/characters/en"

characters <- fromJSON(characters_url)

characters_df <- as.data.frame(characters)

glimpse(characters_df)

#locations
locations_url <- "https://api.api-onepiece.com/v2/locates/en"

locations <- fromJSON(locations_url)

locations_df <- as.data.frame(locations)

glimpse(locations_df)
```
## Description:
This specific dataframe (characters) has most (if not all) the characters from the famous Manga/Anime series
"One Piece". It includes the characters name (ofc) along with their age, size, bounty (in berries), as well what
crew the characters are a part of, & even if they have a devil fruit (as well as what kind with a description).
The dataset is extremely detailed and I am excited to work on it!

## 2 Questions:
- What percentage of the characters have a devil fruit?
- What characters have the highest bounties?

## Plots

```{r}
# characters ages
characters_clean <- characters_df %>%
  unnest_wider(crew, names_sep = ".") %>% 
  mutate(
    age = str_remove(age, " ans"),   # remove the text
    age = as.numeric(age)            # convert to numeric
  )

characters_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(age)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Character Ages",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count"
  )

# east blue vs north blue
blue_counts <- locations_df %>%
  filter(sea_name %in% c("East Blue", "North Blue")) %>%
  count(sea_name)


ggplot(blue_counts, aes(x = sea_name, y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Islands: East Blue vs North Blue",
    x = "Sea",
    y = "Number of Islands"
  )

```
